questions.question:
Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker and mark your answer accordingly on the Answer Sheet. 

I can’t be bothered to tell him what I think;______ he would not listen to me.
questions.solution:
The correct discourse marker is Option 2: in any case.

So the sentence becomes:

I can’t be bothered to tell him what I think; in any case he would not listen to me.

Why?

  • In any case means anyway or even if I did, the result would be the same.

  • Here, the speaker says there is no point in telling him because he would not listen.

Why not the others?

  • meanwhile – used for something happening at the same time.

  • in the meantime – used for the period before something else happens.

  • somehow – means in some way, but it does not fit the meaning here.

Answer: Option 2 – in any case

questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following items in this section consists of a sentence, the parts of which have been jumbled. These parts have been labelled P,  Q, R and S. Given below each sentence have four sequences, namely (a), (b), (c) and (d). You are required to rearrange the jumbled parts of the sentences and mark your response accordingly. 

P. aquaculture nation in the world and 

Q. India’s products are exported to many nations  

R. fish producing and the second largest 

S. India is the third largest
questions.solution:
1. Identify the opening part.

    – S begins with the subject and verb: “India is the third largest …”

    – So S likely comes first.

  2. See what follows “third largest”.

    – R continues: “fish producing and the second largest …”

    – Now we have:

    “India is the third largest fish producing and the second largest …”

  3. Complete the comparison.

    – P adds: “aquaculture nation in the world and …”

    – Together:

    “India is the third largest fish producing and the second largest aquaculture nation in the world and …”

  4. Finish the sentence.

    – Q gives the concluding fact: “India’s products are exported to many nations.”

    – Full sentence:

    “India is the third largest fish producing and the second largest aquaculture nation in the world and India’s products are exported to many nations.”

  5. Sequence of parts: S → R → P → Q, which is Option 1.

Answer: Option 1 (S R P Q)

questions.question:
Consider the following lines of Longitude and Latitude:
1.Prime Meridian
2.Tropic of Cancer
3.Equator
Which of the above lines is/are a Great Circle?
questions.solution:
Consider the following lines of longitude and latitude:

Prime Meridian

Tropic of Cancer

Equator

The correct Great Circle is 3 only, which refers to the Equator.

Key Points

A Great Circle is the largest possible circle on a sphere, dividing it into two equal halves.

The Equator is a Great Circle because it splits the Earth into the Northern and Southern Hemispheres.

The Prime Meridian is not a Great Circle since it merely connects the North and South Poles, not dividing the Earth equally.

The Tropic of Cancer is not a Great Circle; it is a parallel located north of the Equator and does not split the Earth into equal halves.

Therefore, only the Equator is considered a Great Circle among the given options.

Additional Information

Great Circle Definition: Formed when a plane passes through the center of a sphere, creating the largest possible circle on its surface.

Examples of Great Circles: The Equator is the only latitude line that is a Great Circle. All longitudinal lines, like the Prime Meridian, form half of a Great Circle when paired with their opposite meridian.

Importance in Navigation: Great Circles are crucial in navigation and aviation for determining the shortest distance between two points on Earth.

Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn: These are parallels of latitude at 23.5°N and 23.5°S, respectively, and are not Great Circles as they do not equally split the Earth.

Prime Meridian: This line of 0° longitude runs through Greenwich, England, forming only a semicircle by itself and hence is not a Great Circle.

questions.question:
A planet has a mass M1 and radius R1. The value of acceleration due to gravity on its surface is g1.

There is another planet 2, whose mass and radius both are two times that of the first planet. Which one of the following is acceleration due to gravity on the surface of planet 2 ?
questions.solution:
Acceleration due to gravity at planet is given by

$${g_1} = {{G{M_1}} \over {{R_1}^2}}$$ .... (i)

For the second planet,

M2 = 2M1, R2 = 2R1

Now, acceleration due to gravity,

$${g_2} = {{G(2{M_1})} \over {{{(2{R_1})}^2}}} \Rightarrow {g_2} = {1 \over 2} \times {{G{M_1}} \over {{R_1}^2}}$$

From Eq. (i), we get

$${g_2} = {{{g_1}} \over 2}$$

questions.question:
Consider the following statements about the emergence of urban centres in India from circa 6th Century BCE :

1. All of them developed away from the capitals of mahajanapadas.

2. Major towns were located along routes of communication.

3. Many were bustling centres of commercial, cultural and political activity.

How many of the above statements is/are correct ?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each of the given statements about the emergence of urban centres in India from circa 6th Century BCE:

Statement 1: "All of them developed away from the capitals of mahajanapadas."

This statement is not entirely accurate. Some urban centres did develop in proximity to the capitals of mahajanapadas, while others emerged independently. Therefore, this statement is not correct in a blanket sense.

Statement 2: "Major towns were located along routes of communication."

This statement is correct. Many major towns and urban centres were strategically located along important trade routes, which facilitated commerce, communication, and connectivity.

Statement 3: "Many were bustling centres of commercial, cultural, and political activity."

This statement is also correct. Urban centres in ancient India were often hubs of extensive commercial, cultural, and political activities. They thrived on trade and served as focal points for cultural dissemination and political administration.

Based on the above analysis:

Statement 1 is incorrect.

Statements 2 and 3 are correct.

Therefore, the correct option is Option B: 2, as two out of the three statements are correct.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements regarding 'aurora' is/are correct?

1. The solar wind upon reaching the Earth's atmosphere is directed towards two magnetic poles and a colourful display of lights is seen in the night sky.

2. The particles interact with the different gases of the atmosphere and each gas glows with a particular colour.

3. In April 2023, aurora was captured in the night sky in India by the Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh.

Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
The aurora, also known as the northern or southern lights, is a natural light display predominantly seen in high-latitude regions around the Arctic and Antarctic. It is caused by the interaction of charged particles from the solar wind with the Earth's magnetosphere and atmosphere. Let's analyze the given statements :

1. The solar wind upon reaching the Earth's atmosphere is directed towards two magnetic poles and a colourful display of lights is seen in the night sky.

This statement is correct. When the solar wind, which consists of charged particles (mainly electrons and protons), reaches the Earth, its magnetic field directs these particles towards the magnetic poles. As these particles collide with atoms and molecules in the Earth's atmosphere, they excite these atoms and molecules, causing them to emit light. This light emission results in the aurora.

2. The particles interact with the different gases of the atmosphere and each gas glows with a particular colour.

This statement is also correct. Different gases emit different colors when they are excited by the charged particles. For example, oxygen emits green or red light, while nitrogen emits blue or purplish-red light. The specific colors observed in an aurora depend on the types of gas molecules involved and the altitude at which the interactions occur.

3. In April 2023, aurora was captured in the night sky in India by the Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh.

This statement is also correct. In recent times, there have been reports of aurora sightings in regions where they are not commonly seen, due to increased solar activity. In April 2023, the Indian Astronomical Observatory at Hanle, Ladakh, did capture images of aurora in the night sky, making it an unusual but documented event.

Thus, all three statements are accurate. Based on the given options, the correct answer is :

Option D

1, 2 and 3

questions.question:
Which among the following statements with respect to carbon is/are correct?

1. Carbon forms the basis for all living organisms and many things we use

2. Carbon shows tetra-valency and the property of catenation

3. Carbon forms covalent bonds with itself and other elements

4. Carbon forms compounds containing triple and tetra bonds between carbon atoms

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To identify the correct statements regarding carbon, let's analyze each one individually:

Statement 1: Carbon forms the basis for all living organisms and many things we use.

This statement is correct. Carbon is a fundamental element in organic chemistry, forming the backbone of biological molecules such as proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, and nucleic acids. It is also found in numerous materials we use daily, including plastics, fuels, and fibers.

Statement 2: Carbon shows tetra-valency and the property of catenation.

This statement is also correct. Carbon has four valence electrons, allowing it to form up to four covalent bonds with other atoms, which is known as tetra-valency. Catenation refers to the ability of carbon atoms to form long chains or rings with other carbon atoms.

Statement 3: Carbon forms covalent bonds with itself and other elements.

This statement is correct as well. Carbon forms covalent bonds both with other carbon atoms and with different elements like hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, and more, creating a vast array of organic compounds.

Statement 4: Carbon forms compounds containing triple and tetra bonds between carbon atoms.

This statement is incorrect. While carbon can form single, double, and triple bonds with other carbon atoms (e.g., in alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes respectively), it cannot form tetra bonds (or quadruple bonds) in stable compounds. Triple bonds involve one sigma bond and two pi bonds, but quadruple bonds involving four pairs of electrons between two atoms are not common in carbon chemistry due to orbital constraints.

Based on this analysis, the correct statements are 1, 2, and 3.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option C: 1, 2, and 3
questions.subject: General Ability

questions.question: Which one among the. following figures correctly represents the ray diagram ? (Consider the lens to be thin)
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Ray Diagrams for Lenses

A ray diagram is a drawing that shows how light rays move through a lens.

There are three main rays we usually draw for a thin lens:

First Ray: If a ray comes in parallel to the main (principal) axis, it will bend and pass through the lens's focus on the other side.

Second Ray: If a ray goes through the center of the lens, it travels straight and does not bend.

Third Ray: If a ray goes toward the focus before reaching the lens, it will bend after passing through the lens and move out parallel to the main axis.

EXPLANATION:

For a convex lens (one that bulges out):

- A ray that comes in parallel to the main axis will bend and go through the focus on the other side.

- A ray that travels through the middle of the lens goes straight and does not change direction.

- A ray that heads toward the focus before entering the lens will come out of the lens going parallel to the main axis.

So, the right figure for the ray diagram is Option 3.

options: Involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation vomiting ete.
Controlling the regions receiving sensory impulses from receptors
Controlling the movement of voluntary muscles
Sensation of hunger or being full
questions.question: Identify the functions that are controlled by the hind brain of human being
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 1: Involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation vomiting etc.

Let us understand this step-by-step by looking at the divisions of the human brain as described in your NCERT textbook:

  1. The human brain is divided into three major parts or regions: the fore-brain, mid-brain, and hind-brain.

  2. The hind-brain plays a major role in controlling involuntary actions (actions that happen without our conscious thought). It consists of three parts: the pons, medulla, and cerebellum.

According to the NCERT textbook, involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation, and vomiting are strictly controlled by the medulla present in the hind-brain. Therefore, Option 1 is correct.

Let us also look at the other options to understand why they are incorrect:

  • Option 2 (Sensory impulses): The fore-brain is the main thinking part of the brain. It possesses specialized regions that receive sensory impulses from various receptors for hearing, smell, sight, etc.

  • Option 3 (Voluntary muscles): The fore-brain contains motor areas that control the movement of voluntary muscles (like your leg muscles when you decide to walk). While a part of the hind-brain (the cerebellum) helps in the precision and balance of these movements, the actual control and decision of voluntary movement come from the fore-brain.

  • Option 4 (Sensation of hunger): The sensation of feeling hungry or being full is controlled by a separate, specific centre associated with the fore-brain.

Thus, comparing all the functions, Option 1 is the correct function controlled by the hind-brain.

questions.question: In which one among the following situations, the bulb ⊗ would glow the most ? (Consider all batteries are the same)
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Power in a Bulb and Types of Circuits

Power, which makes a bulb glow, is found with the formula:

P = V2 / R

Here, P is power, V is voltage, and R is resistance.

In a series circuit, the battery's voltage is split between all the parts. In a parallel circuit, each part gets the full battery voltage.

EXPLANATION:

The bulb shines the brightest when it gets as much voltage as possible because more voltage means more power (according to the formula above).

If the bulb is in a parallel circuit, it will get the full battery voltage. This makes it glow more.

If the bulb is in a series circuit with other things, the voltage is shared, so the bulb gets less voltage and glows less.

So, the bulb will glow the most if it is connected in parallel.

questions.question:
Directions: Read the following passage carefully and answer the items that follow the passage. Your answers to these items should be based solely on the passage.

Can agricultural productivity meet human needs in the long run? Since twentieth century advances, given increasing costs, do not seem sustainable, further increases in output will not be achieved easily. Global population growth, while showing signs of slowing down, has pushed farmers and herders into areas unsuited to intensive agriculture, accelerating worldwide ecological disruptions. Reserves of arable land and fresh water have diminished alarmingly. Chemical runoffs have compounded pollution problems. Invasion of natural habitats have hastened species extinction. Desertification has been severe in the last hundred years, especially in North Africa, southern portions of Africa, Australia, northern Mexico, and the American Southwest. Tropical forests have shrunk by one-half since World War II, with agricultural pressures causing three-quarters of that loss. Government inter- vention has produced its share of catastrophes, ranging from a terrible famine in China between 1958-1961 that may have killed 30 million people and destroyed the Aral Sea. Taking everything into consideration, we may remain hopeful about humankind's agricultural prospects, but we can scarcely look forward optimistically at the end of the first quarter of the twenty-first century.

Why do twentieth century advances not seem sustainable?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2: Due to the increased cost of agricultural production.

Step-by-step:

  • The passage says: “Since twentieth century advances, given increasing costs, do not seem sustainable…”

  • This clearly tells us that these advances are not sustainable because of increasing costs.

  • So the reason is the increased cost of agricultural production.

Therefore, the answer is:

$$\text{Option 2}$$

questions.question: Which one of the following statements about dihydrogen (H2) is not correct?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement regarding dihydrogen (H₂):

Option A: "H₂ is lighter than air and insoluble in water."

Correct. Hydrogen is indeed lighter than air and has very low solubility in water.

Option B: "H₂ is inert at room temperature due to high H-H bond dissociation enthalpy."

Correct. The H-H bond in H₂ is very strong (with a high dissociation enthalpy, approximately 436 kJ/mol), which makes the molecule relatively unreactive (or inert) under standard conditions unless a catalyst or extreme conditions are provided.

Option C: "H₂ reacts with alkali metals at high temperature to yield metal hydrides."

Correct. Alkali metals can react with hydrogen gas at elevated temperatures to form corresponding metal hydrides (e.g., sodium hydride, NaH).

Option D: "A mixture of NO₂ and H₂ is known as Syngas."

Not Correct. Syngas, short for synthesis gas, is typically a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H₂). A mixture of nitrogen dioxide (NO₂) and H₂ does not constitute syngas.

Therefore, the statement that is not correct is Option D.

questions.question:
Directions: In the following question a pair of similar sounding words is provided. You are required to select the options that most appropriately describes the meaning of both the words and mark your response in the Answer Sheet accordingly.

'Metal' and 'Mettle'
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: 'Metal' is a hard shiny material that is a good conductor, and 'mettle' means mental resilience.

Here's a breakdown of why:

  • Metal: This refers to a type of material that is typically hard, shiny, and a good conductor of heat and electricity. Examples include iron, copper, gold, and silver.
  • Mettle: This word is completely different from 'metal' and refers to a person's strength of character, courage, and determination. It describes someone's ability to withstand challenges and persevere through difficult situations.

Let's analyze why the other options are incorrect:

  • Option A: 'Malleable metal' refers to a metal that can be easily shaped or hammered. While 'mettle' is related to strength, it doesn't specifically mean malleable metal.
  • Option B: 'Metallic substance' is a broader term that encompasses all metals. 'Mettle' doesn't refer to the physical properties of metals.
  • Option C: This option switches the meanings entirely, incorrectly stating that 'metal' means mental resilience and 'mettle' is a type of material.

Therefore, the only option that accurately describes the meanings of 'metal' and 'mettle' is Option D.

questions.question:
Directions : In the following items a sentence or a phrase is given with a word that is underlined, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly
She believed that it was imminent that he would be chosen as the leader of the group.
questions.solution:
The sentence is: "She believed that it was imminent that he would be chosen as the leader of the group."

The word imminent usually means something that is about to happen very soon. It often carries a sense of being close at hand, perhaps even unavoidable.

Now let's look at the options given:

timely: This means happening at the right or most suitable time. It doesn't quite capture the idea of happening very soon.

distant: This means far away in time or space. This is actually the opposite of imminent.

unlikely: This means not likely to happen. Imminent suggests it is going to happen, and soon, so this is also incorrect.

inevitable: This means certain to happen; unavoidable.

When someone believes something is imminent, especially something like being chosen for a role, they often feel it's not just happening soon, but that it's practically guaranteed or unavoidable at that time. Among the options, "inevitable" is the word that best captures this sense of impending certainty that is often part of what makes something seem "imminent". It suggests that the event is so sure to happen that it's right around the corner.

So, the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to imminent in this context is inevitable.

The correct option is Option 4.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements with regard to the constitution of Finance Commission is/are correct?

2. Finance Commission is constituted in pursuance to Article 263 of the Constitution of India.

questions.solution:
Let's analyze the statements given with regard to the constitution of the Finance Commission :

Statement 1 : The Government of India has recently constituted the Sixteenth Finance Commission under the Chairmanship of Dr. Arvind Panagariya.

This statement is incorrect. The Sixteenth Finance Commission has not been constituted under Dr. Arvind Panagariya. The Chairman of the Finance Commission is appointed by the President of India and recent developments would need to be checked for the latest appointment.

Statement 2 : Finance Commission is constituted in pursuance to Article 263 of the Constitution of India.

This statement is also incorrect. The Finance Commission is constituted in pursuance to Article 280 of the Constitution of India, not Article 263. Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D : Neither 1 nor 2

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is correct ?
questions.solution:
Option 2 is correct.

Explanation:

The mass of an electron is

$$m_e \approx 9.11\times10^{-31}\,\text{kg},$$

while that of a proton is

Their ratio is

$$\frac{m_e}{m_p}\approx\frac{1}{1836}\approx\frac{1}{2000}.$$

questions.question: Fundamental Rights guaranteed under which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India is available only to the citizens of India?
questions.solution:
Under Article-19 of the Constitution of India, Fundamental Rights are guaranteed only to the citizens of India. There are five fundamental articles which are only to Indian citizens i.e. Articles-15, 16, 19, 29, 30. This article guarantees the six rights. These are :

1. Right to freedom of speech and expression.

2. Right to assemble peacefully and without arms.

3. Right to form associations or unions or co-operations.

4. Right to move freely throughout the territory of India.

5. Right to reside and settle in any part of the territory of India.

6. Right to practice any profession or to carry on any occupation, trade or business.

While Article 20 deals with the protection of certain rights incase of conviction for offences. Article 21 deals with the protection of life and persons liberty, and Article 22 grants protection to persons who are arrested or detained.

questions.question: Which one of the following rivers in India was not crossed by Alexander and his army?
questions.solution:
Alexander the Great and his army, during their campaign in India, crossed several rivers. Here is a brief explanation of the rivers to determine which one was not crossed by Alexander and his army:

1. Hyphasis: The Hyphasis River is known today as the Beas River. This was the easternmost point of Alexander's campaign in India. It is well documented that his troops refused to march further east of this river, leading him to halt his advance and turn back westward.

2. Acesines: The Acesines River is known today as the Chenab River. Alexander did cross this river on his march through India.

3. Hydraotes: The Hydraotes River is the modern Ravi River. Alexander also crossed this river during his campaign.

4. Hydaspes: The Hydaspes River is known as the Jhelum River today. Alexander fought a famous battle against King Porus on the banks of this river and successfully crossed it.

Based on this information, while Alexander crossed the Acesines (Chenab), Hydraotes (Ravi), and Hydaspes (Jhelum), he did not cross the Hyphasis (Beas) as it was the furthest point of his conquest in India, where his troops refused to proceed further.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: Hyphasis

The Act in question is the Rowlatt Act of 1919. Here’s a quick breakdown:

• Key provision: allowed the government to detain suspects without trial for up to two years

– House-to-house searches without warrant

– Press censorship

• Impact: widespread anger that led to protests (notably the Jallianwala Bagh massacre)

questions.question: The palace of English East India Company settlement in Madras was known as
questions.solution:
The English East India Company was originally known as 'The Company of Merchants of London trading into the East Indies.' It was founded by a Royal Charter, signed by Queen Elizabeth-I, on 31st December, 1600. Sir Thomas Smith was the Company's first Governor.

Soon English open their trading centres at several parts of India. In 1613, Mughal emperor Jahangir issued a farman (permission letter) to English to establish their trade centre in India. Hence, British established their 1st trading factory at Surat in 1613.

In 1639, the East India Company obtained the lease of the city of Madras from the local king where it built Fort St. George to protect its factory. So, the palace of English East India Company settlement in Madras was known as Fort St. George.

questions.question:
When a solid body is partially or completely immersed in a fluid, the fluid exerts an upward force on the body. The magnitude of the force is equal to
1.the mass of the body
2.the weight of the displaced fluid by the body

questions.solution:
The correct choice is 2 only.

By Archimedes’ principle, the buoyant force equals the weight of the fluid displaced:

$$F_{\rm b} = \rho_{\rm fluid}\,V_{\rm disp}\,g$$

where

• ρ₍fluid₎ is the fluid’s density

• V₍disp₎ is the volume of fluid displaced

• g is the acceleration due to gravity.

It is not equal to the mass of the body.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct about NATO ?
questions.solution:
Let's break down each statement about NATO to figure out which one isn't quite right!

Option 1: It was formed out of the signing of the Washington Treaty.

This is absolutely correct! NATO came into being with the signing of the North Atlantic Treaty, also known as the Washington Treaty, on April 4, 1949. This treaty laid out the foundation for the alliance and its principles.

Option 2: Russia is not a member of this organization.

This is also correct. Russia has never been a member of NATO. In fact, NATO was originally formed as a counterweight to the Soviet Union (of which Russia was the largest part) during the Cold War.

Option 3: It derives its authority from the UN Charter.

This statement is NOT correct. While NATO's operations, especially those related to collective self-defense (like Article 5 of the North Atlantic Treaty), are consistent with Article 51 of the UN Charter, which recognizes the inherent right of individual or collective self-defense, NATO does not "derive its authority" directly from the UN Charter. NATO is an independent international organization established by its member states through the North Atlantic Treaty. Its authority comes from that treaty, not from the UN.

Option 4: Finland is its latest member.

This is correct! Finland joined NATO on April 4, 2023, becoming the 31st member of the alliance.

So, the statement that is NOT correct about NATO is Option 3.

This occurs because the soap molecules, which are amphiphilic (having both hydrophilic and hydrophobic parts), align themselves in water to form various ordered structures like micelles and liquid crystalline phases. The formation and ordering of these structures depend on the concentration of the soap in water and the temperature.

Lyotropic liquid crystals are distinguished by their dependence on the concentration of the solute (in this case, soap) in a solvent (water), unlike thermotropic liquid crystals, which depend primarily on temperature.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D : Lyotropic liquid crystal

questions.question: C4H8 belongs to the homologous series of
questions.solution:
To determine the homologous series to which the compound C4H8 belongs, we should first recognize that the general formula for alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and cycloalkenes vary and can be used to classify the compound.

The general formulas are:

Alkanes: $$C_nH_{2n+2}$$

Alkenes: $$C_nH_{2n}$$

Alkynes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Cycloalkenes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Here, the compound C4H8 follows the formula for alkenes:

$$C_4H_8 \implies C_nH_{2n} \text{ where } n = 4$$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: Alkenes

options: that I ask to your advice
that I had asked your advice
that I can have asked your advice
questions.question:
Directions : Each of the following items features one part of a sentence followed by four alternatives. Complete the sentence by choosing the correct alternative. 

Preeti suggests
questions.solution:
Let's analyze the options based on grammatical rules, especially concerning the verb "suggest".

Step 1: Understand the grammatical pattern with 'suggest'.

When the verb "suggest" is used to propose an idea, a plan, or an action, and it is followed by a "that" clause, the verb in the "that" clause typically takes one of two forms:

  1. The base form of the verb (which is the present subjunctive mood).

  2. "should" + the base form of the verb.

For example, if someone suggests an action, we say:

  • "He suggests that I go." (Base form)

  • "He suggests that I should go." ("should" + base form)

Both convey a similar meaning, with the base form being more formal or concise.

Step 2: Evaluate each option based on this pattern.

  • Option 1: that I ask to your advice

    • The phrase "ask to your advice" is grammatically incorrect. We typically "ask for advice" or simply "ask your advice" (where "advice" functions as the direct object). The preposition "to" is misplaced here. So, this option is incorrect.

  • Option 2: that I had asked your advice

    • "had asked" is in the past perfect tense. The main verb "suggests" is in the present tense. If Preeti is suggesting something now, it refers to an action that should happen in the future or is a general recommendation. Using "had asked" implies an action completed in the past before another past event, which doesn't fit the context of a present suggestion. So, this option is incorrect.

  • Option 3: that I can have asked your advice

    • "can have asked" is a modal perfect construction. It expresses a possibility or ability in the past. This tense and modal usage do not fit the context of Preeti making a suggestion for a current or future action. Suggestions are about what should be done, not what could have been done in the past. So, this option is incorrect.

  • Option 4: that I ask your advice

    • This option uses the base form of the verb "ask" ("ask") after "that". This is the grammatically correct subjunctive form used after verbs like "suggest". It implies that "Preeti suggests that I should ask your advice." This fits the pattern perfectly.

Conclusion:

Option 4 is the most appropriate and grammatically correct choice to complete the sentence.

The final answer is $\boxed{\text{Option 4}}$

questions.question: A bullet of mass 10 g is horizontally fired with velocity 300 m s-1 from a pistol of mass 1 kg. What is the recoil velocity of the pistol?
questions.solution:
To determine the recoil velocity of the pistol, we can use the principle of conservation of momentum. According to this principle, the total momentum before and after firing the bullet must be the same because there are no external forces acting on the system.

Given:

  • Mass of the bullet, $ m_b = 10 \, \text{g} = 0.01 \, \text{kg} $
  • Velocity of the bullet, $ v_b = 300 \, \text{m/s} $
  • Mass of the pistol, $ m_p = 1 \, \text{kg} $
  • Recoil velocity of the pistol, $ v_p = ? $

The total momentum before firing is zero because both the bullet and the pistol are at rest initially. Therefore, the total momentum after firing should also be zero.

Let us denote the recoil velocity of the pistol as $ v_p $. The momentum of the bullet and the pistol after firing are given by:

$$ \begin{align*} \text{Momentum of the bullet} & = m_b \cdot v_b \\ \text{Momentum of the pistol} & = m_p \cdot v_p \end{align*} $$

According to the conservation of momentum:

$$ m_b \cdot v_b + m_p \cdot v_p = 0 $$

Solving for $ v_p $:

$$ v_p = -\frac{m_b \cdot v_b}{m_p} $$

Substituting the given values:

$$ \begin{align*} v_p &= -\frac{0.01 \, \text{kg} \times 300 \, \text{m/s}}{1 \, \text{kg}} \\ v_p &= -3 \, \text{m/s} \end{align*} $$

Thus, the recoil velocity of the pistol is $ -3 \, \text{m/s} $. The negative sign indicates that the pistol moves in the opposite direction to that of the bullet.

Hence, the correct option is:

Option C: -3 m s-1

questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: laziness.

Indolence means a disposition to avoid exertion; laziness. The sentence implies that the speaker gave up on the task because they were too lazy to continue.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

Option A: difficulty - Difficulty refers to something being hard to do. While a difficult task might make someone want to give up, it doesn't directly relate to the concept of laziness.

Option C: ugliness - Ugliness refers to physical appearance or something aesthetically displeasing. It has no connection to the act of abandoning a task.

Option D: helplessness - Helplessness refers to being unable to act or help oneself. While someone might feel helpless and abandon a task, it's not the primary meaning of indolence.

questions.question: Sand falls vertically on a conveyor belt at a work rate of 0⋅1 kg/s. In order to keep the belt moving at a uniform speed of 2 m/s, the force required to be applied on the belt is :
questions.solution:
To determine the force required to keep the conveyor belt moving at a uniform speed while sand falls vertically onto it, you can use the relationship between force, mass flow rate, and velocity. Here are the steps to solve this problem:

Mass flow rate of sand:
$ \dot{m} = 0.1 \, \text{kg/s} $

Velocity of the belt:
$ v = 2 \, \text{m/s} $

Momentum change per second (force required):
The force exerted by the falling sand on the belt is equal to the rate of change of momentum of the sand. Since the sand is falling vertically and starts moving horizontally with the belt upon landing, the horizontal force required can be calculated as:

$ F = \dot{m} \cdot v $

Substituting the given values:

$ F = 0.1 \, \text{kg/s} \cdot 2 \, \text{m/s} $

$ F = 0.2 \, \text{N} $

So, the force required to keep the belt moving at a uniform speed of 2 m/s is:

Option B: 0.2 N

questions.solution:
The primary colors of light are identified based on the additive color model, where different colors of light are mixed to create other colors. In this model, the primary colors are:

These colors can be combined in various ways to produce a wide spectrum of colors, including white when all are combined at full intensity. options: The flow of charges between different parts of the cloud

The collection of positively charged particles on the base and collection of negatively charged particles at the top of the cloud
The induction of positive charge on the ground below the negative charge at the base of the cloud
questions.question: Lightning is due to
questions.solution:
Option 1

  1. Kondensation and collision of water‐droplets and ice particles in a cumulonimbus cloud → frictional charging.

  2. Lighter ice crystals (positive) carried to the top, heavier water/ice (negative) settle at the base → large separation of charge.

  3. Potential difference between different regions of the cloud (or between cloud and ground) increases.

  4. When electric field exceeds air’s dielectric strength, a sudden discharge (spark) occurs – this is lightning.

  5. Thus lightning is essentially the rapid flow of charges between oppositely charged parts of the cloud.
